title: Merch Evangelion Figures Superthread 3
wikitext:
[]: [Merch] Evangelion Figures Superthread [3]             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Wed Jun 05, 2013 9:56 pm Continued from http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/3246/Eva-Action-Figures-2/ Evangelion Mark 09 for preorder at AmiAmi ...

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Thu Jun 06, 2013 7:23 am Hhhnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnnqq~ Pre-ordered! And with the quickness too... Thanks for the heads up! Until then, count me out of that one.

[]:             Quote Post by Gojimatt » Thu Jun 06, 2013 10:44 am So only unit 08 to come, then I'm guessing it will go quiet for a while but I wouldn't be surprised if Kaiyodo knock out some alternative 'awakening' versions of unit 09 and 13 before the end of the year... maybe. And hey we get the 'battle cat' unit 02 some way down the line, not that I'm champing at the bit or anything but I'd get one if they did it. I Know I might be starting to sound like a broken record but I can't help but think, looking at unit 09, could Yamaguchi take one more swing at 2.0 body type seeing as he's done such a great job on the very simular uni 09?! Keep dreaming...

[]:             Quote Post by Redtophat » Thu Jun 06, 2013 10:59 am Pre-ordered Mark-09, damn this thing is expensive.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Thu Jun 06, 2013 2:25 pm Mark.09 is awesome and all that but it's rather disappointing that it DIDN'T come with a jetpack. I wanted something that was similar to that cute picture of Rei with a toy Mark.09 that DID include a jetpack.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Fri Jun 07, 2013 9:41 pm Blue Monday wrote: "I am... Disappointed in you..."

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Sun Jun 09, 2013 3:13 pm OK, I may have to get the Q Revoltechs. We probably will get Robot Damashii releases (remember the 1.0 and 2.0 Eva RDs came out in 2010, a full year after the film was first screened) but those figures just look so nice it's a no-brainer. To be honest, having researched Revoltechs, the only things I have against them are the mid-line cut joints they used for the wrists. Kaiyodo, what were you thinking? Even the gloss finish doesn't bother me so much any more-I actually quite like the idea of an Evangelion with a glorious enamel finish, glinting in the sunlight as it strides forwards. Speaking of Kaiyodo, how good are the old Evangelion figures by Kaiyodo (not Revoltechs, this was long before Revoltechs existed)? They certainly LOOK nice, but I don't know how solidly constructed or articulated they are.

[]:             Quote Post by unit450 » Sun Jun 09, 2013 4:50 pm Aurelian wrote: Unit450, It is unlikely they are going to do 00 (nor 03/04). Medicom seems to be interested in producing only the recent and popular Evas, while 00 is the least favorite for what I can see.

[]:             Quote Post by Restriktion » Sun Jun 09, 2013 4:58 pm Squigsquasher wrote: OK, I may have to get the Q Revoltechs. We probably will get Robot Damashii releases (remember the 1.0 and 2.0 Eva RDs came out in 2010, a full year after the film was first screened) but those figures just look so nice it's a no-brainer. To be honest, having researched Revoltechs, the only things I have against them are the mid-line cut joints they used for the wrists. Kaiyodo, what were you thinking? Even the gloss finish doesn't bother me so much any more-I actually quite like the idea of an Evangelion with a glorious enamel finish, glinting in the sunlight as it strides forwards. Speaking of Kaiyodo, how good are the old Evangelion figures by Kaiyodo (not Revoltechs, this was long before Revoltechs existed)? They certainly LOOK nice, but I don't know how solidly constructed or articulated they are.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Sun Jun 09, 2013 5:11 pm Restriktion wrote: They are actually no 1.0 Robot Damashii Evangelion releases - unless you count Unit-01 because it's design was not subject to change - because box-art only mentions the second movie. Having been in possession of a Kaiyodo Unit-02 (End of Evangelion ver.) for around a week, i, speaking personally, won't recommend those releases. They certainly provide good renditions of our favourite characters while being exceptionally tall (about 25 cm) by today's standards. However, their main flaw as i expierienced is one that all action figures share - they are (especially) not built to last. My sample's leg came of after a short amount actual play/pose time and the stands plug broke off after approximately less than 15 attachments. A secondary issue is the restricted poseability, as evident in the arms and legs. Judging from my sample the sculpt is as good as you probably expect from Kaiyodo. But if you plan on being restrictive and exceptionally careful while posing, this figure might be a worthwhile addition to your collection. Ultimately, this is a decision up to you. My opinion is greatly influenced by my negative expierience, and therefore i recommend consulting reviews. Also, be aware that there are more Evangelion figure lines Kaiyodo made before integrating them into their Revoltech lineup. Well, i know i am late. However, i finally managed to upload a picture. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Jun 09, 2013 8:45 pm Yeah, basically that Restriktion ... Try to enlarge the joint peg so that it fits tighter into the hip hole... Squigsquasher gave some really nice suggestions above! Also, week post!

[]:             Quote Post by Gojimatt » Mon Jun 10, 2013 4:18 am Less than a week to go until Unit 08 is released (roughly speaking) and probably the last figure to use the Evo body sculpt. Though I've warmed up to it a bit I wont be sad to see the back of it. Onward to the next gen of revoltech Evangelions!

[]:             Quote Post by Restriktion » Mon Jun 10, 2013 6:02 am Squigsquasher wrote: Thanks. As someone who likes to constantly repose my figures, I'll give the old Kaiyodo figs a miss given the info you've given me. Also, by the looks of things, your problem could be solved quite easily. I have 3 ideas: 1: Floor polish. The most common fix for loose joints, never tried it myself but it's supposed to work well. 2: Gloss varnish. I've used this for a while (I use Games Workshop varnish, as I paint Warhammer 40,000 models and usually have some left over). It works quite well. 3: Super Glue. There are 2 ways to using this; A: Apply Super Glue to the peg, dab it with a tissue, repeat until you have a thick coating of Super Glue on the peg. B: Apply a small amount of Super Glue to the peg and re-attach the leg. Then swivel and rotate the joint as much as possible until all the glue has set, removing the leg if necessary. This will make the joint much, much tighter, but you must be very careful not to let it actually glue the pieces together-I broke a Jazwares Sonic the Hedgehig figure like that (although that figure used this horrible rubbery plastic with floppy hinge joints, rather than the high-quality ABS plastic with unpluggable peg and socket joints Kaiyodo uses). I used it to tighten up my RD Unit 00's waist and arms, and it worked a treat.

[]:             Quote Post by Gojimatt » Mon Jun 10, 2013 7:02 am I was simply using Evo as way of referring to the base body type used for the majority of figures in this line thus far (Unit 01 uses essentially the same body type as most of the other Eva Evos the shoulder pylons, chest and obviously the head being the only difference) as a way of differentiating them from their 3.0 brethren. Apologies if my terminology was a little loose.

[]:             Quote Post by shaneva002 » Mon Jun 10, 2013 8:36 pm Gojimatt wrote: Less than a week to go until Unit 08 is released (roughly speaking) and probably the last figure to use the Evo body sculpt. Though I've warmed up to it a bit I wont be sad to see the back of it. Onward to the next gen of revoltech Evangelions!

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Wed Jun 12, 2013 5:09 am Eva-01 // Mark.06 Revo box set arrived today. Will probably take some snaps over the weekend. Seele-01: You weren't wrong, buddy. It is a very cool package with that sliding feature!

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Jun 12, 2013 11:41 am Squigsquasher wrote: How good are the original Kaiyodo (not Revoltech) Evangelion figures? They certainly LOOK good, but I don't know if they're well made, well articulated, etc...

[]:             Quote Post by fleeting » Wed Jun 12, 2013 7:39 pm Squigsquasher wrote: How good are the original Kaiyodo (not Revoltech) Evangelion figures? They certainly LOOK good, but I don't know if they're well made, well articulated, etc...

[]:             Quote Post by Theron » Thu Jun 13, 2013 12:52 am There's a good amount of shots of Unit 13 on Yamaguchi's twitter- he likes to post pictures of about-to-be-released revoltechs in shenanigans. https://twitter.com/fuikitodoki

[]:             Quote Post by Gojimatt » Thu Jun 13, 2013 7:11 am Sweet find!
